---
title: "Apples vs. Oranges"
subtitle: "A comparison framework"
author: "Your Name"
date: "June 2026"
format:
  html:
    theme: ../apples-oranges.scss
    toc: true
    code-block-bg: true
---

[Design System]{.eyebrow}

## Why compare?

Two options, one decision. Each side gets its own visual voice, and the
contrast keeps a reader oriented — the same layout every time, only the
palette flips.

::: {.callout-note title="Note"}
The apple tone uses pale text on a solid navy ground; callouts sit on a
desaturated navy-tint panel.
:::

## Strengths & trade-offs

| Metric      | Apples              | Oranges              |
|-------------|---------------------|----------------------|
| Strength    | Crisp texture       | High vitamin C       |
| Strength    | Long shelf life     | Segment-friendly     |
| Trade-off   | Less juice per bite | Peel can be messy    |

## Neutral ground

::: {.scheme-banana}
Neutral blocks sit outside the apples / oranges pairing — good for
summaries, agenda, or shared context.
:::

```python
# three tones
scheme = ["apples", "oranges", "banana"]
```
